title: Talking About the Future
description: Use 'going to' for plans and 'will' for predictions and spontaneous decisions.

items.example: I'm going to study tonight. (ya lo decidí)
It will rain tomorrow. I'll help you!
I'm going to visit my parents this weekend.
She's going to start a new job.
Don't worry, I'll do it for you.
It won't take long. They won't be late.

items.front: Will you…? / Are you going to…?
items.example: Will you help me? Are you going to come?

items.prompt: I already bought the tickets. We ___ see the concert on Friday.
items.explanation: Already bought tickets = a decided plan → 'going to'. We are going to see the concert.

items.prompt: The phone is ringing! — Don't worry, I ___ answer it.
items.explanation: Spontaneous decision at the moment of speaking → 'will'. I'll answer it.

items.prompt: Look at those clouds. It ___ rain.
items.explanation: Evidence visible now (dark clouds) = strong prediction with 'going to'.

items.prompt: She ___ be late — she always arrives on time. (prediction)
items.explanation: General prediction without visible evidence → 'won't'. She won't be late.

items.prompt: ___ you help me move this table?
items.explanation: Asking for a favour on the spot → 'Will you…?' Will you help me?

activities.title: Complete with going to or will

items.sentence: I ___ study Spanish tomorrow. I've already planned it.
She's tired. She ___ go to the party tonight.
I ___ get you a coffee — I'm just going to the kitchen.
Look at him run! He ___ win the race.
___ you be at the meeting tomorrow?
